message:
feat(mvcc): implement adaptive conflict policy with expected-loss selection

Add ConflictPolicy enum (Strict, SafeMerge, Adaptive) governing how
MVCC commit handles FCW conflicts. SafeMerge is the default, matching
the merge-proof behavior added in the previous commit.

Adaptive mode uses an expected-loss decision model:
  E[loss] = P(corruption) * severity + P(unnecessary_abort) * cost

Introduce AdaptivePolicyConfig with tunable parameters (corruption
severity, abort cost, base corruption probability, EMA alpha, warmup
commits) and ContentionMetrics tracking conflict rate, merge success
rate, and abort rate via exponential moving averages.

Wire the policy into ShardedMvccStore:
- set_conflict_policy() / conflict_policy() / effective_policy() API
- preflight_fcw_locked() now checks effective policy before attempting
  merge resolution — Strict mode aborts immediately on any conflict
- contention_metrics are updated on every commit (success, conflict,
  merge, abort) for adaptive decision-making
- contention_metrics() exposes runtime observability

Add 8 unit tests covering: expected-loss calculation, warmup fallback,
Strict-mode conflict abort, Adaptive switching between Strict and
SafeMerge, contention metrics EMA tracking, serde roundtrip, and
default policy verification.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
